categories.title: General Plug-In Modules
categories.description: Self-contained modules (30–45 min each) that can be swapped into the main program based on audience needs.

modules.title: Conflict as a Leadership Tool
modules.purpose: Most managers avoid conflict. The best leaders use it. This module reframes conflict from something to be managed to something to be leveraged—and gives participants tools for navigating difficult conversations with skill.
learningObjectives: Understand the five conflict-handling modes and when each is appropriate
Reframe conflict as a source of better decisions and stronger relationships
Navigate difficult conversations using a structured preparation framework
Practice opening lines that create dialogue rather than defensiveness

keyTopics.title: Thomas-Kilmann Conflict Modes
keyTopics.description: Five modes (Competing, Collaborating, Compromising, Avoiding, Accommodating) and when each is appropriate. Most leaders have a default—the question is whether it matches the situation.

keyTopics.title: Difficult Conversation Preparation
keyTopics.description: Five-question framework: What happened? What story am I telling myself? What's their story? What do I want? How can I start? Enter with curiosity, not conclusions.

keyTopics.title: Opening Lines That Work
keyTopics.description: Replace confrontational openings with curiosity-based ones. Instead of 'We need to talk' try 'I'd like to understand your perspective on...'

modules.title: Leading Hybrid & Remote Teams
modules.purpose: Addresses the unique challenges of leading when you can't rely on physical presence. Builds trust and inclusion without proximity, and designs effective async/sync communication rhythms.
learningObjectives: Understand the key challenges of hybrid/remote leadership
Build trust and inclusion without physical presence
Design effective asynchronous and synchronous communication rhythms
Avoid common hybrid pitfalls like proximity bias

keyTopics.title: The Hybrid Leadership Challenge
keyTopics.description: What changes when you can't see your team: casual check-ins must be intentional, output-based trust replaces visible effort, proximity bias unconsciously favors in-person people.

keyTopics.title: Async-First Operating Rhythm
keyTopics.description: Default to asynchronous communication; reserve synchronous time for what truly requires real-time interaction. A sample weekly rhythm balancing written updates with meetings.

keyTopics.title: Inclusion Practices
keyTopics.description: If one person is remote, everyone joins virtually. Rotate meeting times. Assign a 'remote advocate.' Document decisions in writing.

modules.title: Managing Up & Across
modules.purpose: Expands on managing-up content for audiences where peer relationships and upward influence are primary challenges. Covers boss mapping, upward feedback, and building peer alliances.
learningObjectives: Build a strategic relationship with your manager
Give feedback upward without damaging trust
Build peer alliances across departments
Navigate situations where your boss's style conflicts with yours

keyTopics.title: Boss Mapping Exercise
keyTopics.description: Map your boss's top pressures, information preferences, stress triggers, what they value in reports, and where they feel exposed. Manage your boss the way THEY want to be managed.

keyTopics.title: COIN Framework for Upward Feedback
keyTopics.description: Context (set the stage) → Observation (what you noticed, no judgment) → Impact (the effect observed) → Next (a suggestion or question). Frame as helping the team.

keyTopics.title: Peer Network Audit
keyTopics.description: Rate relationships with 5-7 key peers from 1-5. Strategies: find shared interests, offer help first, create informal touchpoints, share information generously, give credit publicly.

modules.title: Building Your Leadership Pipeline
modules.purpose: Helps leaders think systematically about developing the next generation using the 9-Box Grid and 70-20-10 development model.
learningObjectives: Assess team readiness and identify high-potential individuals
Create development opportunities beyond formal training
Build succession depth across your team
Apply the 'What happens when you leave?' test

keyTopics.title: The 9-Box Grid
keyTopics.description: Map team members on Performance vs. Potential: Develop (invest in growth), Stretch (give bigger challenges), Address (performance conversation), Retain (keep engaged and growing).

keyTopics.title: 70-20-10 Development Model
keyTopics.description: 70% from on-the-job experience (stretch assignments, leading projects), 20% from learning from others (mentoring, peer learning), 10% from formal learning (training, courses).

keyTopics.title: Development Actions
keyTopics.description: Delegate leadership moments, create 'acting' opportunities, pair junior with senior, debrief together after key moments, share your thinking (explain WHY, not just what).

modules.title: Emotional Intelligence in Practice
modules.purpose: Deepens self-awareness content for emotional regulation, empathy, and reading interpersonal dynamics. Covers Goleman's four EI domains and trigger mapping.
learningObjectives: Understand the four domains of emotional intelligence
Identify your emotional triggers and develop regulation strategies
Practice empathy as a strategic leadership tool
Read the emotional temperature of a room

keyTopics.title: Goleman's Four EI Domains
keyTopics.description: Self-Awareness (knowing your triggers), Self-Regulation (managing emotional responses), Social Awareness (reading others and the room), Relationship Management (using awareness to guide interactions).

keyTopics.title: Trigger Mapping & The 6-Second Pause
keyTopics.description: Map your triggers and typical reactions. Between trigger and response, there's a gap—emotional intelligence is what you do in that gap. Name the emotion → Pause → Choose your response.

keyTopics.title: Reading the Room
keyTopics.description: Before any important meeting: What's the energy? Who's engaged? What's not being said? What emotion am I bringing? Observing what's below the surface separates good leaders from great ones.

modules.title: Strategic Prioritization & Saying No
modules.purpose: Builds the discipline of prioritization using the Eisenhower Matrix—and the courage to say no (or 'not now') to protect team capacity and strategic focus.
learningObjectives: Apply the Eisenhower Matrix to current workload
Distinguish between 'urgent' and 'important' in daily practice
Say no to requests without damaging relationships
Protect team capacity from initiative overload

keyTopics.title: The Eisenhower Matrix
keyTopics.description: Four quadrants: Do First (urgent + important), Schedule (important + not urgent), Delegate (urgent + not important), Eliminate (not urgent + not important). The best leaders spend disproportionate time in the top-right quadrant.

keyTopics.title: The Art of Saying No
keyTopics.description: Five approaches: The Redirect, The Trade-off, The Delay, The Scope Reduction, The Honest Boundary. Each preserves relationships while protecting capacity.

keyTopics.title: Protecting Team Capacity
keyTopics.description: Initiative overload is a leadership failure, not a team failure. Leaders must actively protect focus and say 'not now' to preserve strategic priorities.

modules.title: Cross-Cultural Leadership
modules.purpose: Builds awareness of cultural dimensions (Hofstede, Meyer) that affect leadership style, communication, and decision-making across international contexts.
learningObjectives: Understand key cultural dimensions that impact leadership
Adapt leadership style across cultural contexts
Navigate common cross-cultural misunderstandings
Leverage cultural diversity as a strategic advantage

keyTopics.title: Cultural Dimensions
keyTopics.description: Based on Hofstede and Erin Meyer: Power Distance, Communication Style, Time Orientation, Individualism/Collectivism, Uncertainty Avoidance. No 'right' culture—only different defaults.

keyTopics.title: Cross-Cultural Friction Points
keyTopics.description: Direct feedback, silence after proposals, missed deadlines, decisions without meetings—each interpreted differently across cultures. The bridge skill: 'Is this a personality issue or a cultural difference?'

keyTopics.title: Turkey in Context
keyTopics.description: Higher power distance than Northern Europe, high-context communication, strong collective loyalty, moderate uncertainty avoidance with entrepreneurial flexibility.

categories.title: HR Leadership Modules
categories.description: Designed for leadership trainings delivered to HR teams—addressing the unique challenges HR leaders face.

modules.title: The HR Leader as 'Leader of Leaders'
modules.purpose: Addresses the distinct challenge of coaching and influencing managers across the organization without formal authority. Builds credibility as a strategic leadership partner.
learningObjectives: Understand the unique influence dynamics of the HR leadership role
Coach managers who don't report to you without becoming 'HR police'
Build credibility as a strategic leadership partner
Navigate the tension between advising and directing

keyTopics.title: The HR Influence Paradox
keyTopics.description: HR leaders are expected to shape leadership org-wide but rarely have positional authority. The influence-without-authority challenge at its most extreme.

keyTopics.title: The Credibility Equation for HR
keyTopics.description: Shift from 'HR says we have to...' to 'Here's the business risk if we don't...' Build trust through business language and strategic framing.

keyTopics.title: Coaching Without Authority
keyTopics.description: Practical techniques for influencing and developing managers who don't report to you—building trust through questions, not directives, and earning a seat at the table through impact.

modules.title: Leading Through Organizational Transformation
modules.purpose: HR leaders don't just support organizational change—they architect it. Addresses the unique emotional and strategic burden of leading restructurings and transformations.
learningObjectives: Lead organizational change as architect, not just supporter
Manage the emotional burden of driving restructurings
Apply change frameworks to HR-led transformations
Support your own team while leading others through change

keyTopics.title: The Dual Burden of HR Change Leadership
keyTopics.description: HR simultaneously designs the change and holds people through it—often without anyone holding HR through it.

keyTopics.title: Change Architecture
keyTopics.description: Building on Module 4's change equation, specific strategies for HR-led transformations: restructurings, culture changes, and AI-driven role redesign.

keyTopics.title: Self-Care in Change Leadership
keyTopics.description: Who supports HR during change? Building resilience practices and peer support structures when you're simultaneously designing and enduring transformation.

modules.title: The Culture Architect — Building Culture by Design
modules.purpose: Moves HR leaders from describing culture to actively shaping it. Connects psychological safety frameworks to intentional culture design.
modules.bestFor: HR leaders focused on culture transformation
learningObjectives: Move from describing culture to actively shaping it
Connect psychological safety frameworks to culture design
Build intentional culture through systems and behaviors
Measure and maintain culture over time

keyTopics.title: Culture by Design
keyTopics.description: Culture can't be left to chance. HR leaders must intentionally build, maintain, and sometimes repair organizational culture.

keyTopics.title: The Culture Stack
keyTopics.description: Connecting psychological safety (Module 3) and change management (Module 4) to systematic culture architecture.

keyTopics.title: Measuring Culture
keyTopics.description: Moving beyond engagement surveys to track culture health: behavioral indicators, decision pattern analysis, and leading indicators that predict cultural drift before it becomes crisis.

modules.title: Data-Driven HR — People Analytics for Influence
modules.purpose: Builds capability to use people data as forward-looking business storytelling rather than backward-looking reporting.
learningObjectives: Translate people data into business insight
Use data as forward-looking storytelling, not backward-looking reporting
Build influence at the leadership table through analytics
Connect people metrics to business outcomes

keyTopics.title: From Reporting to Storytelling
keyTopics.description: Shift from 'here are the numbers' to 'here's what the numbers mean for business strategy.' Predict, don't just react.

keyTopics.title: People Analytics for Influence
keyTopics.description: Using data to gain disproportionate influence—connecting directly to Module 2's influence and stakeholder mastery frameworks.

keyTopics.title: The Metrics That Matter
keyTopics.description: Not all data is created equal. Identifying the 3–5 people metrics that directly connect to your organization's strategic priorities—and having the discipline to ignore the rest.

modules.title: Navigating the Dual Role — Employee Advocate & Business Partner
modules.purpose: Addresses the tension between advocating for employees and serving the business—one of the most adaptive challenges in the HR profession.
modules.bestFor: HR leaders balancing employee and business needs
learningObjectives: Navigate the tension between employee advocacy and business partnership
Apply adaptive leadership to HR's defining challenge
Make values-based decisions when employee and business needs conflict
Build trust with both employees and business leaders

keyTopics.title: The Adaptive Challenge of HR
keyTopics.description: No technical solution to the advocate/partner tension—only judgment, values, and the ability to hold competing commitments.

keyTopics.title: Decision Navigation
keyTopics.description: Connecting Module 1's adaptive leadership framework to HR's most complex decisions: when to advocate, when to partner, when to push back.

keyTopics.title: Building Trust on Both Sides
keyTopics.description: Practical strategies for maintaining credibility with employees and business leaders simultaneously—even when decisions appear to favor one over the other.

categories.title: Finance Leadership Modules
categories.description: Designed for leadership trainings delivered to Finance teams—addressing the unique challenges finance leaders face.

modules.title: Financial Storytelling — Turning Numbers into Influence
modules.purpose: Builds the skill of translating financial data into compelling stories that influence strategy. The #1 emerging skill gap for finance leaders.
modules.bestFor: Finance leaders who need to influence beyond numbers
learningObjectives: Translate financial data into compelling strategic narratives
Influence decision-making through data storytelling
Present numbers in ways that drive action
Connect financial insights to business strategy

keyTopics.title: Numbers vs. Narrative
keyTopics.description: Accuracy alone doesn't drive decisions—narrative does. The critical emerging skill for finance leaders.

keyTopics.title: Strategic Communication for Finance
keyTopics.description: Building on Module 2's influence frameworks: how to present financial data to different audiences in ways that drive strategic decisions.

keyTopics.title: The Visual Argument
keyTopics.description: Designing financial presentations that drive decisions: simplify complex data, highlight the 'so what,' and create visual narratives that executives remember and act on.

modules.title: From Scorekeeper to Strategic Partner
modules.purpose: Addresses the leadership shift from gatekeeper to enabler. The traditional finance role is being automated—the future belongs to strategic partners.
modules.bestFor: Finance leaders seeking strategic partnership role
learningObjectives: Shift from gatekeeper to enabler role
Partner with business leaders to drive decisions
Build trust as a strategic advisor
Move beyond tracking and controlling to enabling

keyTopics.title: The Finance Leadership Shift
keyTopics.description: Traditional finance (tracking, reporting, controlling) is being automated. The future belongs to finance leaders who partner with business.

keyTopics.title: Building Partnership Trust
keyTopics.description: Using the Trust Equation from Module 2 to transform finance's relationship with business stakeholders.

keyTopics.title: Proactive Business Engagement
keyTopics.description: Moving from waiting to be asked to anticipating business needs. The shift from reactive reporting to proactive strategic insight that positions finance as an indispensable partner.

modules.title: Leading Through Cost Pressure & Difficult Trade-offs
modules.purpose: Addresses the psychological and leadership dimensions of being the bearer of difficult news while leading your own team through constraints.
learningObjectives: Lead teams through resource constraints while maintaining motivation
Communicate difficult financial decisions effectively
Balance cost discipline with team morale
Navigate the 'bearer of bad news' role

keyTopics.title: The Leadership Burden
keyTopics.description: Finance leaders must drive cost discipline org-wide while simultaneously leading their own team through constraints.

keyTopics.title: Communicating Difficult Decisions
keyTopics.description: Building on Module 4's ownership framework: how to deliver hard messages with accountability and empathy.

keyTopics.title: Team Resilience Under Constraint
keyTopics.description: Maintaining team morale and engagement when the message is 'do more with less.' Practical tools for protecting motivation and preventing cynicism during austerity.

modules.title: Building a Future-Ready Finance Team
modules.purpose: Helps finance leaders build teams that can thrive in 2026—navigating shrinking talent pipelines and expanding skill requirements.
learningObjectives: Build teams for the evolving finance landscape
Address the talent pipeline challenge
Develop AI fluency within finance teams
Create development paths for emerging capabilities

keyTopics.title: The Finance Talent Squeeze
keyTopics.description: Shrinking pipeline of graduates while required skills expand: data analytics, AI fluency, business partnering.

keyTopics.title: Future-Ready Development
keyTopics.description: Connecting to Plug-in D (Leadership Pipeline) and Module 5 (AI Fluency) for finance-specific team building.

keyTopics.title: AI Fluency in Finance
keyTopics.description: Building practical AI literacy across finance teams—from automated reconciliation to predictive analytics—without losing the human judgment that makes finance valuable.

modules.title: Risk, Uncertainty & Decision Leadership
modules.purpose: Builds capability to frame uncertainty, model scenarios, and help business leaders make decisions with incomplete information.
learningObjectives: Frame uncertainty for business leaders
Model scenarios for strategic decision-making
Move beyond risk reporting into decision leadership
Help leaders make decisions with incomplete information

keyTopics.title: From Risk Reporting to Decision Leadership
keyTopics.description: Finance leaders are uniquely positioned to navigate uncertainty—but only by moving beyond reporting.

keyTopics.title: Scenario Modeling & Decision Frameworks
keyTopics.description: Extending Module 4's RAPID and bias mitigation frameworks into finance-specific strategic contexts.

keyTopics.title: Communicating Uncertainty
keyTopics.description: How to present incomplete information without undermining confidence. The art of saying 'we don't know yet' while still providing leadership, direction, and decision frameworks.

categories.title: Engineering Leadership Modules
categories.description: Designed for leadership trainings delivered to Engineering teams—addressing the unique challenges engineering managers face.

modules.title: The IC-to-Manager Transition
modules.purpose: Addresses the identity shift from individual contributor to manager. What made you successful as an engineer can actively undermine you as a manager.
modules.bestFor: New engineering managers; recently promoted ICs
learningObjectives: Navigate the identity shift from IC to manager
Redefine 'productivity' for the management role
Avoid common traps that derail new engineering managers
Build a new leadership identity beyond technical expertise

keyTopics.title: The Identity Shift
keyTopics.description: What made you successful as an engineer—deep focus, technical mastery, solving problems yourself—can actively undermine you as a manager.

keyTopics.title: New Engineering Manager Traps
keyTopics.description: The hero coding trap, the 'just let me do it' instinct, measuring productivity by code output instead of team output. Building on Module 1's self-awareness.

keyTopics.title: Redefining Productivity
keyTopics.description: Your output is no longer code—it's the team's output. Practical frameworks for measuring your impact through team velocity, capability growth, and unblocking others rather than personal contribution.

modules.title: Translating Engineering to Business
modules.purpose: Builds the critical skill of translating engineering reality into business impact. Technical debt, infrastructure investment, and refactoring in business language.
learningObjectives: Communicate engineering value in business language
Frame technical needs as business impact
Influence resource allocation through strategic communication
Build bridges between engineering and business stakeholders

keyTopics.title: The Translation Gap
keyTopics.description: Technical debt sounds optional, infrastructure investment sounds like cost, 'we need to refactor' sounds like rework. Reframing in business impact language.

keyTopics.title: Influence Through Translation
keyTopics.description: Extending Module 2's influence frameworks: how to communicate engineering reality to executives, product, and business teams.

keyTopics.title: The Business Case Toolkit
keyTopics.description: Practical templates for framing engineering investments: ROI of reliability, cost of delay, risk quantification. Turn 'we need to refactor' into 'this reduces outage risk by 40% and saves €2M annually.'

modules.title: Engineering Team Health — Burnout & Sustainable Pace
modules.purpose: Gives engineering managers practical tools for monitoring and maintaining team health amid on-call pressure, AI anxiety, and 'always shipping' culture.
modules.bestFor: Engineering teams facing burnout and pressure
learningObjectives: Monitor team health indicators proactively
Address burnout before it becomes critical
Create sustainable pace in high-pressure environments
Navigate AI anxiety within engineering teams

keyTopics.title: Engineering-Specific Burnout Risks
keyTopics.description: On-call rotations, incident response, 'always shipping' culture, AI reshaping profession. Extending Module 1's energy management and Module 3's psychological safety.

keyTopics.title: Sustainable Pace Tools
keyTopics.description: Practical monitoring and maintenance tools for engineering managers facing the burnout crisis.

keyTopics.title: Navigating AI Anxiety
keyTopics.description: AI is reshaping the engineering profession in real time. Addressing team fears about obsolescence, reframing AI as an amplifier of engineering judgment, and creating psychological safety around adoption and experimentation.

modules.title: Managing Technical Debt as a Leadership Challenge
modules.purpose: Frames technical debt management as an adaptive leadership challenge requiring cross-functional negotiation and strategic prioritization.
learningObjectives: Frame technical debt as a leadership challenge
Negotiate cross-functionally for debt reduction
Prioritize debt strategically using business impact
Communicate debt risks to non-technical stakeholders

keyTopics.title: Debt as Adaptive Challenge
keyTopics.description: Technical debt requires cross-functional negotiation, stakeholder communication, and strategic prioritization—not just engineering effort.

keyTopics.title: The Debt Prioritization Framework
keyTopics.description: Connecting Module 1's Heifetz framework, Module 2's influence skills, and Module 4's ownership tools to technical debt decisions.

keyTopics.title: Communicating Debt to Non-Technical Stakeholders
keyTopics.description: Translating technical debt into business language: 'Every sprint we spend 30% on workarounds' becomes 'we're losing a third of our capacity to deliver new features.' Making the invisible visible to drive action.

modules.title: Leading Engineering Through AI Transformation
modules.purpose: Addresses specific AI challenges engineering leaders face—between executive expectations of 10x productivity and the messy reality of integration.
learningObjectives: Navigate the gap between AI expectations and reality
Manage team anxiety about AI disruption
Build practical AI adoption strategies
Balance executive expectations with engineering reality

keyTopics.title: The AI Leadership Gap
keyTopics.description: Between executive expectations of 10x productivity and the messy reality of integration challenges, quality concerns, and team anxiety.

keyTopics.title: Engineering-Specific AI Strategy
keyTopics.description: Extending Module 5's AI fluency into the specific challenges of AI-assisted coding, quality concerns, and team adoption in engineering.

keyTopics.title: Managing the Expectations Gap
keyTopics.description: Executives hear '10x productivity' while engineers experience debugging AI-generated code. Building credibility by setting realistic milestones, demonstrating incremental wins, and honest reporting on what AI can and can't do today.

modules.title: The Dual Ladder — Supporting ICs & Managing Managers
modules.purpose: Addresses the dual career ladder (IC vs management track) and the fundamentally different job of managing other managers.
learningObjectives: Support both IC and management career tracks
Navigate the challenges of managing other managers
Build a healthy dual career ladder in your organization
Develop both technical and people leaders

keyTopics.title: The Dual Career Ladder
keyTopics.description: Some best people want to stay technical (IC track), others want to lead people (management track). Supporting both is essential.

keyTopics.title: Managing Managers
keyTopics.description: A fundamentally different job from managing ICs—connecting to Plug-in D (Leadership Pipeline) and Module 3's coaching framework.

keyTopics.title: Retention & Growth Across Both Tracks
keyTopics.description: Top ICs leave when the only path to recognition is management. Top managers burn out without peer support. Designing equitable compensation, visibility, and growth opportunities that retain talent on both tracks.
